description_long: This is a regional jet airliner painted in the Delta Air Lines livery, featuring a streamlined white fuselage with a blue and red tail fin. The aircraft has a low-wing configuration with slightly swept-back wings and small winglets at the tips. Two turbofan engines are mounted at the rear of the fuselage, just ahead of the T-tail, which consists of a horizontal stabilizer mounted atop a single vertical fin. The nose section is rounded with a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit featuring multiple windows. The landing gear is of the tricycle type, with the main gear under the wings and a single nose wheel visible. The overall design is typical of modern regional jets used for short to medium-haul flights.
description: A sleek, white regional jet with Delta Air Lines livery, low-swept wings, rear-mounted engines, and a T-tail design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the Delta livery, low-mounted swept wings, rear-mounted engines, and T-tail.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the twin rear-mounted engines, T-tail, and winglets at the wingtips.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with landing gear and cockpit windows visible.
description_view_3: Front view showing the rounded nose, cockpit windows, and the full wingspan with slight dihedral angle.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with moderately swept wings and small upward winglets at the tips; slight dihedral angle present.
attribute_tail_design: T-tail configuration with a single vertical stabilizer and a horizontal stabilizer mounted at the top.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear with two main gear units and a single nose gear.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, typical of a multi-seat commercial jet.
